---
title: "Drafting Space"
format: html
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Pseudo Code

Lexer
Character by character

For after each normal character,
If a special character or space, then the running "read" is a token, else keep reading

After a special character, if it was a paren or dot that's it, else if a lambda then continue reading for the full token.

Parser
Token by token
( makes new branch
) ends and returns a level
*should handle main dependency-levelling, just needs left and right association for application and abstraction, respectively. I don't need the tree to be binary or efficient hypothetically, so long as evaluation can be done step by step using pairs.

/ denotes a binding token, drop / to read name
. denotes a separation, shouldn't be needed, drop . to process semantics, term after binding is bound so no symbol needed.

Binding token and term token together should be the abstraction to apply to another term.


Tree visualizing library in R?

(X(y))
((X)y)






```{r}
#| echo: false
#The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
```
